title: Asteranthemum vulgare

The family Asteraceae is commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family, consisting of over 32,000 known species. It is native to certain regions, although the exact native range is not specified in available information. Further research would be needed to determine the precise classification of Asteranthemum vulgare.

intro: Asteranthemum vulgare, commonly known as the bitter clover or bitter clover plant, is a plant species in the legume family. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including its ability to aid digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties.
